pi_items.source: Task.json and export_result.sh verification - standard Linux desktop path used in task setup and export scripts

OpenICE official documentation (openice.info) and IMPACT dataset documentation confirm both device types exist in OpenICE simulators. Export script checks for 'multiparameter' and 'infusion.*pump' patterns in device detection.

Oracle Java documentation (docs.oracle.com) and jcmd utility documentation confirm java.vm.name is a standard JVM system property returned by 'jcmd <PID> VM.system_properties' command that identifies the JVM implementation (e.g., 'Java HotSpot(TM) 64-Bit Server VM')
Oracle Java documentation confirms java.version is a standard JVM system property returned by jcmd VM.system_properties that identifies the JDK/JRE version being used

Oracle Java documentation and jcmd utility documentation confirm os.name is a standard JVM system property returned by 'jcmd <PID> VM.system_properties' that identifies the operating system (e.g., 'Linux', 'Windows 10')

privileged_info_summary: The task uses OpenICE, a real open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program (openice.info). The required devices - Multiparameter Monitor and Infusion Pump - are confirmed simulators available in OpenICE's demo applications. The required JVM properties (java.vm.name, java.version, os.name) are standard Java system properties returned by the jcmd diagnostic tool via the 'VM.system_properties' command, which is part of the Java Development Kit. The task expects the audit output file at /home/ga/Desktop/deployment_audit.txt, a standard Linux desktop file path. The environment uses Java 17 (OpenJDK 17) as configured in setup_openice.sh.
